expectations.evidence: The workflow does not include an explicit complexity classification step. It jumps directly into understanding the dependency graph without labeling the task as Simple/Medium/Complex.
The workflow is structured as 9 detailed steps with validation criteria at each phase, covering dependency analysis, protocol extraction, service refactoring, DI wiring, testing, linting, and commit.
No mention of worktree isolation, branching strategy, or isolation of any kind. The workflow proceeds directly without worktree creation.
No sub-agent delegation or model selection is mentioned. The workflow is described as a single agent performing all steps sequentially.
Step 7 includes: Run `scripts/lint-deps` (dependency direction checker) if available ... Run `scripts/lint-quality` if available ... Run a full build
Step 7: Run `scripts/lint-deps`, Run `scripts/lint-quality`, Run a full build: `xcodebuild build`, Run `scripts/validate.py`

Step 8 describes a code review checklist but does not delegate to a different model or sub-agent. It is a self-review checklist: 'Review checklist: Protocol methods match original service signatures exactly...'
No mention of checkpoints, phase-boundary saves, or harness/trace/checkpoints/ at any point in the workflow.
No mention of trajectory compilation, procedural memory, success counting, or pattern compilation anywhere in the workflow.
